narration: After sorting, we start with the current interval [1, 3].
callout: The first interval becomes the current range.

narration: The next interval [2, 6] overlaps because 2 starts before 3 ends, so we merge into [1, 6].
decision: Overlap means extend the current end.

narration: When [8, 10] starts after 6, there is no overlap. We save [1, 6] and start a new current interval.
callout: No overlap means save the current interval and move on.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Why can sorting place possible merge partners next to each other?
